context.projectOverview: agent-bober multi-provider harness. This sprint improves DX for the optional `openai` peer dependency: (1) verify (with a test) the existing call-time hard error in openai.ts that fires when import('openai') fails, and (2) add a preflight check that, when a role is configured to an openai-family provider (openai, openai-compat, or a deepseek shorthand that resolves to openai-compat) but the openai package is not installed, emits an actionable hint containing 'npm install openai' BEFORE the first model call — a warning/hint, never a hard abort.

completedSprints.title: DeepSeek resolution + key
completedSprints.summary: deepseek/-v4-pro/-v4-flash resolve to openai-compat @ https://api.deepseek.com via resolveProviderModel; DEEPSEEK_API_KEY env fallback + DeepSeek-specific missing-key error in validateApiKey (endpoint threaded as 4th param). openai client mocked in tests via vi.doMock.

principles: ESM (.js import extensions, NodeNext). consistent-type-imports (`import type`). no-explicit-any warned (unknown+narrowing). Zero type + lint errors are hard gates. No SDK lock-in: NEVER import the openai package outside provider adapter files — the preflight must DETECT openai presence via a dynamic import('openai') wrapped in try/catch (mirroring openai.ts getClient), NOT a static import. Small utility modules. Use logger from src/utils/logger.ts. Tests mock the dynamic import (vi.mock/vi.doMock). Conventional commit: bober(sprint-3): description.

baseline.note: 2 pre-existing environmental vitest TIMEOUT flakes on this branch (mcp 'registers exactly 37 tools' tests) — baseline, ignore, they pass in isolation.

notesForGenerator: Stay on branch spike/claude-code-provider. Follow the contract generatorNotes + the briefing. Key design points: (1) The preflight must be a WARNING/HINT, never a hard error that aborts startup (nonGoal). (2) Detect openai-family roles by scanning each configured role's model/provider through resolveProviderModel — deepseek shorthands resolve to openai-compat, so they count. anthropic-only config must emit NOTHING. (3) Detect openai presence by attempting import('openai') and catching rejection — make this mockable in tests (a thin wrapped helper you can vi.mock, or accept an injectable importer for testability). (4) Do NOT change the existing call-time error wording in openai.ts — only add a TEST for it. (5) Do NOT add openai to package.json dependencies. Add tests for: missing-package throw (import mocked to reject), preflight hint when openai-family + openai absent, preflight silent when openai present, preflight silent for anthropic-only. Verify build + lint exit 0 before committing.
